description: The inverter stops due to GFDI protection triggered by excessive negative-to-ground voltage.

steps.title: Check Parameter Settings
steps.description: In the UMCG interface, verify that the negative grounding mode is set to 'Controllable'. Check that the negative-to-ground voltage protection threshold and protection time are correctly configured.
steps.question: Are the parameter settings correct? (yes / no)
steps.no: Adjust the parameter settings and recheck the system.

steps.title: Check Fuse FU15
steps.description: With the inverter powered off, use a multimeter in continuity mode to check whether fuse FU15 is intact.
steps.question: Is fuse FU15 functioning properly? (yes / no)

steps.title: Check Contactor KM5
steps.description: Inspect the KM5 contactor in the negative grounding protection circuit. Confirm whether it is properly energized (pulled in).
steps.question: Is the KM5 contactor operating correctly? (yes / no)

steps.title: Check Resistor R3
steps.description: With the inverter powered off, measure the resistance of R3 using a multimeter. The expected value is approximately 1kΩ.
steps.question: Is the resistance of R3 within the expected range (~1kΩ)? (yes / no)

steps.title: Check PL1 Board Wiring
steps.description: Inspect the wiring connections on the PL1 board. Ensure that all cables are properly connected and not loose or abnormal.
steps.question: Is the PL1 board wiring normal? (yes / no)
steps.no: Fix the wiring issues on the PL1 board.

steps.title: Replace or Swap PL Board
steps.description: If a spare PL board is available, replace it and observe whether the inverter returns to normal operation. Alternatively, swap with a working inverter for verification.
steps.question: Does the inverter recover after replacing/swapping the PL board? (yes / no)
steps.yes: The original PL board is faulty and should be replaced.

steps.title: Check DC Side Voltage to Ground
steps.description: Turn off the DC switch and measure the voltage from both positive and negative terminals to ground. The voltage should drop from approximately 650V.
steps.question: Is the DC voltage to ground within the normal range? (yes / no)
steps.no: Isolate the fault by disconnecting combiner boxes or checking individual DC strings.

steps.title: Check AC Side Grounding
steps.description: Check whether there is any unintended grounding on the AC side (e.g., reactor grounding).
steps.question: Is there any abnormal grounding on the AC side? (yes / no)
steps.yes: Locate and fix the grounding issue.
steps.no: Escalate the issue to advanced technical support.
